subject: Re: Bayesian Polishing on Warp-extracted particles
body:
*Update:*
*When I try to run a bayesian polishing job in relion3 (source code was 
updated today) according to the instructions (*
http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=371)* I get the following errors: *

"in: /opt/em/relion3b/relion-3.0_beta/src/jaz/micrograph_handler.cpp, line 
529
=== Backtrace  ===
/opt/em/relion3b/relion-3.0_beta/build/bin/relion_motion_refine(_ZN11RelionErrorC2ERKNSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIcSt11char_traitsIcESaIcEEES7_l+0x6d) 
[0x42591d]
/opt/em/relion3b/relion-3.0_beta/build/bin/relion_motion_refine(_ZN17MicrographHandler11getMetaNameENSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIcSt11char_traitsIcESaIcEEE+0x1f2) 
[0x560942]
/opt/em/relion3b/relion-3.0_beta/build/bin/relion_motion_refine(_ZN17MicrographHandler11loadInitialERK13MetaDataTabledbRiRdRNSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIcSt11char_traitsIcESaIcEEE+0x17d) 
[0x561a6d]
/opt/em/relion3b/relion-3.0_beta/build/bin/relion_motion_refine(_ZN17MicrographHandler4initERK13MetaDataTabledbiRiRdRNSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIcSt11char_traitsIcESaIcEEE+0x99) 
[0x562d49]
/opt/em/relion3b/relion-3.0_beta/build/bin/relion_motion_refine(_ZN13MotionRefiner4initEv+0x4f4) 
[0x45d5a4]
/opt/em/relion3b/relion-3.0_beta/build/bin/relion_motion_refine(main+0x44) 
[0x423284]
/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6(__libc_start_main+0xf0) [0x7f2d667c9830]
/opt/em/relion3b/relion-3.0_beta/build/bin/relion_motion_refine(_start+0x29) 
[0x423e09]
==================
ERROR: 
ERROR: MicrographHandler::getMetaName: no metadata star-file for 
FoilHole_17742552_Data_17734310_17734311_20190121_1015-120070.mrc found in 
NAD-3-part-1.star."

*However the paths in the micrograph list from Warp seem correct. Same for 
the paths in the metadata-containing .star files in the 'motion' folder:*

*Relevant line in the micrograph list (NAD-3...star) from Warp (this is in 
the relion directory, together with the raw movies): *

I'm sure I'm missing something stupid, but any help would be very much 
appreciated.

Cheers,
Tom
Op dinsdag 22 januari 2019 11:33:33 UTC schreef tom dendooven:
>
> Hi Dimitry,
>
> A very quick question: After exporting the micrographs list from Warp for 
> Bayesian polishing in Relion3, would one also need the raw movies to do the 
> polishing (besides a decent map, particles.star file and the particle 
> stacks), or would the averaged images from Warp somehow suffice? I couldn't 
> find any paths to the raw movies in the star files but may have overlooked 
> them, hence my question.
>
> Many thanks,
> Tom
>
>
>
